name: terminal-state-manager
description: Saves and restores your terminals (Claude Code sessions, docker/podman/kubectl log watchers, SSH sessions, plain shells) across VS Code restarts.

commands.title: Terminal Manager: Save Terminal State Now
Terminal Manager: Restore Terminal State Now
Terminal Manager: Clear Saved Terminal State

terminalManager.enabled.description: Enable terminal state tracking and restore for this workspace. Off by default -- having a saved terminal-state.json and the extension installed is not enough on its own; each project must opt in explicitly via this workspace setting.

terminalManager.autoRestoreOnStartup.description: Automatically restore saved terminals when a workspace finishes loading.

terminalManager.restoreDelayMs.description: Delay (ms) after startup before restoring terminals, to let the window settle.

terminalManager.restoreStaggerMs.description: Delay (ms) between creating each restored terminal, so restoring many terminals at once doesn't spawn all of their processes in one burst. Negligible for a handful of terminals; smooths out the spike for dozens.

terminalManager.claudeCorrelation.enable.description: Attempt to correlate Claude Code terminals with their exact session ID for --resume. When disabled, Claude terminals always restore via the interactive picker.

terminalManager.debugLogging.description: Also write debug logs to .vscode/terminal-manager.log (in addition to the always-on 'Terminal Manager' Output channel). Off by default -- this file grows unboundedly with no rotation, so only turn it on while actively diagnosing an issue.
